meta.title: CogDB — A Second Brain for AI Agents
meta.description: The only database that gives AI agents episodic, semantic, and procedural memory — all in one engine. One install, one interface, one data store.
meta.author: Mustapha Liaichi

hero.headline: A Second Brain for AI Agents
hero.subheadline: The only database that gives AI agents episodic, semantic, and procedural memory — all in one engine.

problem.headline: Every AI agent framework treats memory as someone else's problem.
problem.body: You bolt on a vector database. Then a graph DB for relationships. Then SQLite for structured stuff. Now you're maintaining three backends that don't talk to each other — and when two agents update the same fact at the same time, things break silently.

points.title: Three backends, zero coordination
points.description: Vector DB + graph DB + SQLite — maintained separately, queried separately, breaking separately.

points.title: Context window blowouts
points.description: No token management means every recall dumps a wall of context into the LLM and hopes for the best.

points.title: Silent data loss in multi-agent systems
points.description: Concurrent agent writes with no conflict resolution. Facts overwrite each other. Nobody notices.

solution.headline: One engine. Three memory types. Zero glue code.

memory_types.name: Episodic
memory_types.description: Timestamped records of agent interactions, observations, and tool calls. Stored as vector embeddings with full metadata. Agents search by similarity — "find me situations like this one."

memory_types.name: Semantic
memory_types.description: A temporal knowledge graph where facts have lifecycles. Facts get superseded automatically when newer ones contradict them. Confidence scores, validity windows, provenance links.

memory_types.name: Procedural
memory_types.description: Learned workflows captured from successful agent task completions. Reusable templates with EMA success tracking. This memory type basically doesn't exist in any other system.

comparison_table.headline: Why CogDB
comparison_table.subheadline: Most memory systems bolt on vector search. CogDB was designed from scratch for the full cognitive stack.

benchmark.label: Tri-Memory Retrieval Benchmark
benchmark.description: Suite 1 measures episodic + semantic + procedural recall quality across a mixed workload. 140 tests pass. Score improved from 87.9 → 90.7 with the learned ImportanceModel (Ridge regression).

token_retrieval.headline: Token-aware retrieval that actually works.
token_retrieval.body: Most memory systems dump a wall of context into the LLM and hope for the best. CogDB fills your token budget from the top with the highest-importance memories that fit. Faster responses. Lower API costs. No context window blowouts.

integrations.headline: Works with every major AI framework

features.title: Rust storage engine
features.description: Purpose-built HNSW vector index, WAL crash recovery, and petgraph knowledge graph — all behind a clean Python API.

features.title: Learned importance model
features.description: Ridge regression on access patterns ranks memories by relevance. No external API key required.

features.title: 4-scope multi-agent isolation
features.description: Private, team, org, and session scopes with contradiction detection at write time. No silent data loss.

features.title: Schema migration
features.description: Versioned add/rename/drop/change_type with metadata backfill. db.migrate_schema() keeps your data consistent across schema changes.

features.title: MCP server built-in
features.description: cogdb-mcp CLI exposes 6 tools to any MCP-compatible agent — Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and more.

features.title: Zero cold-start overhead
features.description: Singleton engine per db_path. No connection pools, no warmup. Just open and use.

roadmap.summary: Tri-memory engine, pipeline, AutoGen + LangGraph + MCP adapters.
Purpose-built storage with HNSW, WAL, crash recovery, PyO3 bindings.
Learned importance model, HNSW rank blending, tokenised procedure search. Score: 90.7/100.
Typed schemas (3A), migration tooling (3C), metadata indexing (3B in progress).

cta_footer.headline: Ready to give your agents a real memory?
cta_footer.subheadline: One install. Three memory types. Zero backends to manage.
